sourceTitle: Teamspaces give every team a home for important work

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a teamspace in Notion?

options.explanation: Teamspaces are meant for teams, not personal note‑taking.

options.text: Provide a dedicated area for a team to organize and access information
options.explanation: Exactly – a teamspace is a workspace‑within‑a‑workspace that centralises a team’s content.
A teamspace lives inside a workspace; it does not replace it.
Billing is handled at the workspace level, not by individual teamspaces.

questions.question: Which teamspace is created by default and includes everyone in the workspace?

options.explanation: Marketing is a possible custom teamspace, not the default.
The default, all‑members teamspace is named "General".
"Private" describes a type of teamspace, not the default name.
"Open" is a permission setting, not a teamspace name.

questions.question: How can a user join a teamspace they are not yet a member of?

options.text: Hover over the teamspace in All teamspaces and click “Join”
options.explanation: The guide specifies this as the method to join a teamspace.

options.text: Email Notion support and request access
options.explanation: Support isn’t required for a normal join operation.
Only owners can edit settings; regular members must join first.

options.text: They cannot join without an invitation
options.explanation: Only Closed teamspaces require an invitation; Open ones can be joined freely.

questions.question: When creating a new teamspace, which step ensures that all workspace members are automatically added?

options.explanation: Enabling this option adds everyone in the workspace to the new teamspace automatically.

options.text: Add each member manually in the Members tab
options.explanation: Manual addition works but does not automatically include everyone.

options.text: Set the teamspace permission to Private
options.explanation: Private limits visibility; it does not auto‑add members.

options.text: Choose an icon for the teamspace
options.explanation: Choosing an icon is cosmetic and unrelated to membership.

questions.question: Which statement about Open, Closed, and Private teamspaces is true?

options.text: Private teamspaces are available on all Notion plans
options.explanation: Private teamspaces are an Enterprise‑only feature.

options.text: Closed teamspaces allow anyone to view content without joining
options.explanation: Closed teamspaces are visible but cannot be viewed without an invitation.

options.text: Open teamspaces let anyone join and view content by default
options.explanation: Open teamspaces are fully accessible to anyone who joins; they’re the recommended default.

options.text: Closed teamspaces are only visible to owners
options.explanation: Closed teamspaces are visible to everyone, but joining requires an invite.

questions.question: In the Permissions tab of a teamspace, which access level allows a member to edit a page but not share it externally?

options.explanation: Full access also permits sharing the page.
Can edit lets the user modify the page while restricting external sharing.
Can comment only allows commenting, not editing.
Can view provides read‑only access.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a recommended top‑level page type to place in a teamspace sidebar?

options.explanation: A homepage/wiki is a core navigation page.
Important documents like roadmaps belong at the top level.
Read‑only reference material is encouraged as a top‑level page.
Personal to‑do lists belong in a private area, not a shared teamspace sidebar.

questions.question: What is the maximum recommended number of default teamspaces for a large workspace?

options.explanation: One is the minimum, but the guide allows up to three.
Two is permissible but not the upper recommendation.
The article recommends no more than three default teamspaces, even for large orgs.
Unlimited defaults would create clutter and is not advised.

questions.question: An Enterprise plan user wants to prevent guests from being invited to pages within a specific private teamspace, while the workspace overall allows guest invites. Which setting provides this granular control?

options.text: Toggle “Allow guests to share pages” in the Security tab of the teamspace
options.explanation: The Security tab (Enterprise only) lets owners override workspace‑level guest settings for that teamspace.
That setting only affects default membership, not guest permissions.
Closed restricts joining but does not control guest invites on individual pages.
Changing workspace‑level settings would affect all teamspaces, not just one.

questions.question: A user reports that no teamspaces appear in their sidebar. Which combination of factors is most likely causing this issue?

options.text: They are on a Personal Plan and their app is outdated
options.explanation: Personal plans don’t support teamspaces, and an outdated app can prevent the new sidebar from loading.

options.text: They have disabled the sidebar in settings
options.explanation: The sidebar cannot be disabled; it’s a core navigation element.
Archived teamspaces would still show a collapsed section; the guide mentions archiving only removes them from view, not the entire sidebar.

options.text: They are using a mobile device that does not support teamspaces
options.explanation: Teamspaces are available on mobile; the issue is plan or app version, not device type.
